description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped rock with a layered, stratified appearance, featuring a mix of muted brown, gray, and off-white tones. The surface is rugged and uneven, with visible ridges and crevices running along its length, suggesting sedimentary origins. One end of the rock tapers off into a thinner, sheet-like extension, while the opposite end is more blocky and compact. The top surface is relatively flat but not smooth, with patches of lighter coloration and some shallow depressions. The sides display pronounced textural variation, with some areas appearing more eroded or chipped. This rock would be a prominent feature in an urban park or landscaped area, serving as a natural barrier or decorative element.
description: A large, layered sedimentary rock with rugged, uneven surfaces and a mix of brown, gray, and off-white colors. It features a flat top, pronounced ridges, and a distinctive sheet-like extension on one end.
description_view_0: The rock is seen from the side, highlighting its elongated, blocky form and the flat, slightly sloped top surface with visible color patches.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the tapered, sheet-like extension and the pronounced stratification along the side, with more visible surface texture.
description_view_2: The underside and layered structure are most visible here, showing deeper crevices and a darker coloration in the shadowed areas.
description_view_3: The compact, blocky end of the rock is prominent, with the flat top and layered sides visible, and the extension mostly hidden.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
